title: sambucus racemosa f. ovatifolia
intro: Sambucus racemosa f. ovatifolia, also known as the Blue Elderberry or Red-berried Elder, is a deciduous shrub native to western North America. The plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Sambucus racemosa f. ovatifolia include reducing symptoms of the common cold and flu, alleviating pain and inflammation, and supporting cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and capsules, and can also be used topically to treat skin issues and wounds.
